revision.text:
{{神社| 名称=久久美雄彦神社| 画像=| 所在地=岐阜県養老郡養老町沢田1341| 祭神=久久美雄彦神| 社格=元郷社| 創建=不明| 本殿=| 例祭=10月8日| 神事=| }}
'''久久美雄彦神社'''（くくみおひこじんじゃ）は、[[岐阜県]][[養老郡]][[養老町]]にある[[神社]]である。[[延喜式神名帳|式内社]]の[[美濃国]][[多芸郡]]久久美雄彦神社であり、旧[[社格]]は[[郷社]]。久々美雄彦神社とも表記される。
[[養老山地]]の北端近くの中腹にあり、[[東海自然歩道]]沿いである。
== 沿革 == 創建時期は不明。[[仁明天皇]]より[[承和 (日本)|承和]]5年（[[838年]]）従五位下の神階を授けられたということが[[続日本後紀]]に記述されていることから、[[平安時代]]初期以前の創建と推測される。
[[戦国時代 (日本)|戦国時代]]の頃は久々見穗彦大明神、[[江戸時代]]は久々美雄彦神社大明神と呼称されていた記述がある。[[明治]]6年（[[1873年]]）に郷社となった際に久久美雄彦神社に改称している。
== 祭神 == *久久美雄彦神 :久久美雄彦神はどのような神かは不明。以下の説がある。 #[[伊富岐神社]]の多多美彦命と同一神。但し伊富岐神社の祭神も[[八岐大蛇]]、[[天火明命]]、[[ウガヤフキアエズ|草葦不合尊]]などの諸説あるのだが、多多美彦命（夷服岳神、気吹男神、伊富岐神ともいう）は[[伊吹山]]の神とされている。 #天戸辺尊の分神である久久美穂彦神。天戸辺尊の詳細は不明であるが、[[オオトノヂ・オオトノベ|大戸辺尊]]と推測される。
== 境内社 == *三社神社 （[[イザナギ|伊邪那岐神]]・[[イザナミ|伊邪那美神]]・[[コノハナノサクヤビメ|木花咲屋姫神]] ） *神明神社 （[[アマテラス|天照大御神]]）
== 所在地 == *岐阜県養老郡養老町沢田1341
{{DEFAULTSORT:くくみおひこしんしや}} [[Category:岐阜県の神社]] [[Category:式内小社]] [[Category:郷社]] [[Category:養老町]] {{shinto-stub}}

revision.text:
{{神社| 名称=久久美雄彦神社| 画像=| 所在地=岐阜県養老郡養老町沢田1341| 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|19|23.46|N|136|31|23.52|E|scale:20000}}| 祭神=久久美雄彦神| 社格=式内社（小）、元郷社| 創建=不明| 本殿=| 例祭=10月8日| 神事=| }}
'''久久美雄彦神社'''（くくみおひこじんじゃ）は、[[岐阜県]][[養老郡]][[養老町]]にある[[神社]]である。[[延喜式神名帳|式内社]]の[[美濃国]][[多芸郡]]久久美雄彦神社であり、[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は[[郷社]]。久々美雄彦神社とも表記される。
[[養老山地]]の北端近くの中腹にあり、[[東海自然歩道]]沿いである。
== 沿革 == 創建時期は不明。[[仁明天皇]]より[[承和 (日本)|承和]]5年（[[838年]]）従五位下の神階を授けられたということが[[続日本後紀]]に記述されていることから、[[平安時代]]初期以前の創建と推測される。
[[戦国時代 (日本)|戦国時代]]の頃は久々見穗彦大明神、[[江戸時代]]は久々美雄彦神社大明神と呼称されていた記述がある。[[明治]]6年（[[1873年]]）に郷社となった際に久久美雄彦神社に改称している。
== 祭神 == *久久美雄彦神 :久久美雄彦神はどのような神かは不明。以下の説がある。 #[[伊富岐神社]]の多多美彦命と同一神。但し伊富岐神社の祭神も[[八岐大蛇]]、[[天火明命]]、[[ウガヤフキアエズ|草葦不合尊]]などの諸説あるのだが、多多美彦命（夷服岳神、気吹男神、伊富岐神ともいう）は[[伊吹山]]の神とされている。 #天戸辺尊の分神である久久美穂彦神。天戸辺尊の詳細は不明であるが、[[オオトノヂ・オオトノベ|大戸辺尊]]と推測される。
== 境内社 == *三社神社 （[[イザナギ|伊邪那岐神]]・[[イザナミ|伊邪那美神]]・[[コノハナノサクヤビメ|木花咲屋姫神]] ） *神明神社 （[[アマテラス|天照大御神]]）
== 所在地 == *岐阜県養老郡養老町沢田1341
{{DEFAULTSORT:くくみおひこしんしや}} [[Category:岐阜県の神社]] [[Category:式内小社]] [[Category:岐阜県の旧郷社]] [[Category:養老町]] {{shinto-stub}}

{{神社 |名称=久久美雄彦神社 |画像=[[File:Kukumiohiko shrine 2.jpg|300px]] |所在地=岐阜県養老郡養老町沢田1341 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|19|23.46|N|136|31|23.52|E|scale:20000}} |祭神=久久美雄彦神 |社格=式内社（小）、元郷社 |創建=不明 |本殿= |例祭=10月8日 |神事= |}}
'''久久美雄彦神社'''（くくみおひこじんじゃ）は、[[岐阜県]][[養老郡]][[養老町]]にある[[神社]]である。[[延喜式神名帳|式内社]]の[[美濃国]][[多芸郡]]久久美雄彦神社であり、[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は[[郷社]]。久々美雄彦神社とも表記される。
[[養老山地]]の北端近くの中腹にあり、[[東海自然歩道]]沿いである。
== 沿革 == 創建時期は不明。[[仁明天皇]]より[[承和 (日本)|承和]]5年（[[838年]]）従五位下の神階を授けられたということが[[続日本後紀]]に記述されていることから、[[平安時代]]初期以前の創建と推測される。
[[戦国時代 (日本)|戦国時代]]の頃は久々見穗彦大明神、[[江戸時代]]は久々美雄彦神社大明神と呼称されていた記述がある。[[明治]]6年（[[1873年]]）に郷社となった際に久久美雄彦神社に改称している。
== 祭神 == *久久美雄彦神 :久久美雄彦神はどのような神かは不明。以下の説がある。 #[[伊富岐神社]]の多多美彦命と同一神。但し伊富岐神社の祭神も[[八岐大蛇]]、[[天火明命]]、[[ウガヤフキアエズ|草葦不合尊]]などの諸説あるのだが、多多美彦命（夷服岳神、気吹男神、伊富岐神ともいう）は[[伊吹山]]の神とされている。 #天戸辺尊の分神である久久美穂彦神。天戸辺尊の詳細は不明であるが、[[オオトノヂ・オオトノベ|大戸辺尊]]と推測される。
== 境内社 == *三社神社 （[[イザナギ|伊邪那岐神]]・[[イザナミ|伊邪那美神]]・[[コノハナノサクヤビメ|木花咲屋姫神]] ） *神明神社 （[[アマテラス|天照大御神]]）
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}

{{nihongo|'''Kukumiohiko Shrine'''|久久美雄彦神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Mino Province]].

== instance of (P31) ==

{{nihongo|'''Kukumiohiko Shrine'''|久久美雄彦神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Mino Province]].

== instance of (P31) ==

{{nihongo|'''Kukumiohiko Shrine'''|久久美雄彦神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Mino Province]].

== instance of (P31) ==

{{Infobox religious building | name = Kukumiohiko Shrine | native_name = 久久美雄彦神社 | image = Kukumiohiko shrine 2.jpg | mapframe = yes | mapframe-zoom = 15 | mapframe-wikidata = yes | mapframe-point = none | map_type = Japan | map_caption = Kukumiohiko Shrine | coordinates = {{coord|35.323162|136.523104}} | map_relief = 1 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] }}
{{nihongo|'''Kukumiohiko Shrine'''|久久美雄彦神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Mino Province]].

{{Infobox religious building | name = Kukumiohiko Shrine | native_name = 久久美雄彦神社 | image = Kukumiohiko shrine 2.jpg | mapframe = yes | mapframe-zoom = 15 | mapframe-wikidata = yes | mapframe-point = none | map_type = Japan | map_caption = Kukumiohiko Shrine | coordinates = {{coord|35.323162|136.523104}} | map_relief = 1 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] }}
{{nihongo|'''Kukumiohiko Shrine'''|久久美雄彦神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Mino Province]].

revision.comment: 9 revisions imported
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称=久久美雄彦神社 |画像=[[File:Kukumiohiko shrine 2.jpg|250px]]
鳥居 |所在地=[[岐阜県]][[養老郡]][[養老町]]沢田1341 |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神=久久美雄彦神 |社格=[[式内社]]（小）論社、旧[[郷社]] |創建=不明 |本殿= |例祭=10月8日 |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Gifu|label=久久美雄彦
神社|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''久久美雄彦神社'''（くくみおひこじんじゃ）は、[[岐阜県]][[養老郡]][[養老町]]にある[[神社]]である。[[延喜式神名帳|式内社]]の[[美濃国]][[多芸郡]]久久美雄彦神社であり、[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は[[郷社]]。久々美雄彦神社とも表記される。 [[養老山地]]の北端近くの中腹にあり、[[東海自然歩道]]沿いである。 == 沿革 == 創建時期は不明。[[仁明天皇]]より[[承和 (日本)|承和]]5年（[[838年]]）従五位下の神階を授けられたということが[[続日本後紀]]に記述されていることから、[[平安時代]]初期以前の創建と推測される。 [[戦国時代 (日本)|戦国時代]]の頃は久々見穗彦大明神、[[江戸時代]]は久々美雄彦神社大明神と呼称されていた記述がある。[[明治]]6年（[[1873年]]）に郷社となった際に久久美雄彦神社に改称している。 == 祭神 == *久久美雄彦神 :久久美雄彦神はどのような神かは不明。以下の説がある。 #[[伊富岐神社]]の多多美彦命と同一神。但し伊富岐神社の祭神も[[八岐大蛇]]、[[天火明命]]、[[ウガヤフキアエズ|草葦不合尊]]などの諸説あるのだが、多多美彦命（夷服岳神、気吹男神、伊富岐神ともいう）は[[伊吹山]]の神とされている。 #天戸辺尊の分神である久久美穂彦神。天戸辺尊の詳細は不明であるが、[[オオトノヂ・オオトノベ|大戸辺尊]]と推測される。 == 境内社 == *三社神社　（[[イザナギ|伊邪那岐神]]・[[イザナミ|伊邪那美神]]・[[コノハナノサクヤビメ|木花咲屋姫神]] ） *神明神社　（[[アマテラス|天照大御神]]） == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:くくみおひこしんしや}} [[Category:岐阜県の神社]] [[Category:美濃国の式内社]] [[Category:岐阜県の旧郷社]] [[Category:養老町の歴史]] [[Category:養老町の建築物]]

[[Category:Shikinaisha in Mino Province]] [[JA:久久美雄彦神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11368919}} [[Category:Junior Fifth Rank]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] {{translated page|ja|久久美雄彦神社|version=107307596|comment=Imported full ja history}}[[Category:Automerged Japanese text]] == Japanese Wikipedia content == {{神社 |名称=久久美雄彦神社 |画像=[[File:Kukumiohiko shrine 2.jpg|250px]]
鳥居 |所在地=[[岐阜県]][[養老郡]][[養老町]]沢田1341 |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神=久久美雄彦神 |社格=[[式内社]]（小）論社、旧[[郷社]] |創建=不明 |本殿= |例祭=10月8日 |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Gifu|label=久久美雄彦
神社|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''久久美雄彦神社'''（くくみおひこじんじゃ）は、[[岐阜県]][[養老郡]][[養老町]]にある[[神社]]である。[[延喜式神名帳|式内社]]の[[美濃国]][[多芸郡]]久久美雄彦神社であり、[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は[[郷社]]。久々美雄彦神社とも表記される。 [[養老山地]]の北端近くの中腹にあり、[[東海自然歩道]]沿いである。 == 沿革 == 創建時期は不明。[[仁明天皇]]より[[承和 (日本)|承和]]5年（[[838年]]）従五位下の神階を授けられたということが[[続日本後紀]]に記述されていることから、[[平安時代]]初期以前の創建と推測される。 [[戦国時代 (日本)|戦国時代]]の頃は久々見穗彦大明神、[[江戸時代]]は久々美雄彦神社大明神と呼称されていた記述がある。[[明治]]6年（[[1873年]]）に郷社となった際に久久美雄彦神社に改称している。 == 祭神 == *久久美雄彦神 :久久美雄彦神はどのような神かは不明。以下の説がある。 #[[伊富岐神社]]の多多美彦命と同一神。但し伊富岐神社の祭神も[[八岐大蛇]]、[[天火明命]]、[[ウガヤフキアエズ|草葦不合尊]]などの諸説あるのだが、多多美彦命（夷服岳神、気吹男神、伊富岐神ともいう）は[[伊吹山]]の神とされている。 #天戸辺尊の分神である久久美穂彦神。天戸辺尊の詳細は不明であるが、[[オオトノヂ・オオトノベ|大戸辺尊]]と推測される。 == 境内社 == *三社神社　（[[イザナギ|伊邪那岐神]]・[[イザナミ|伊邪那美神]]・[[コノハナノサクヤビメ|木花咲屋姫神]] ） *神明神社　（[[アマテラス|天照大御神]]） == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} [[Category:岐阜県の神社]] [[Category:美濃国の式内社]] [[Category:岐阜県の旧郷社]] [[Category:養老町の歴史]] [[Category:養老町の建築物]] ==End Japanese==

鳥居 |所在地=[[岐阜県]][[養老郡]][[養老町]]沢田1341 |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神=久久美雄彦神 |社格=[[式内社]]（小）論社、旧[[郷社]] |創建=不明 |本殿= |例祭=10月8日 |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Gifu|label=久久美雄彦
神社|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''久久美雄彦神社'''（くくみおひこじんじゃ）は、[[岐阜県]][[養老郡]][[養老町]]にある[[神社]]である。[[延喜式神名帳|式内社]]の[[美濃国]][[多芸郡]]久久美雄彦神社であり、[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は[[郷社]]。久々美雄彦神社とも表記される。 [[養老山地]]の北端近くの中腹にあり、[[東海自然歩道]]沿いである。 == 沿革 == 創建時期は不明。[[仁明天皇]]より[[承和 (日本)|承和]]5年（[[838年]]）従五位下の神階を授けられたということが[[続日本後紀]]に記述されていることから、[[平安時代]]初期以前の創建と推測される。 [[戦国時代 (日本)|戦国時代]]の頃は久々見穗彦大明神、[[江戸時代]]は久々美雄彦神社大明神と呼称されていた記述がある。[[明治]]6年（[[1873年]]）に郷社となった際に久久美雄彦神社に改称している。 == 祭神 == *久久美雄彦神 :久久美雄彦神はどのような神かは不明。以下の説がある。 #[[伊富岐神社]]の多多美彦命と同一神。但し伊富岐神社の祭神も[[八岐大蛇]]、[[天火明命]]、[[ウガヤフキアエズ|草葦不合尊]]などの諸説あるのだが、多多美彦命（夷服岳神、気吹男神、伊富岐神ともいう）は[[伊吹山]]の神とされている。 #天戸辺尊の分神である久久美穂彦神。天戸辺尊の詳細は不明であるが、[[オオトノヂ・オオトノベ|大戸辺尊]]と推測される。 == 境内社 == *三社神社　（[[イザナギ|伊邪那岐神]]・[[イザナミ|伊邪那美神]]・[[コノハナノサクヤビメ|木花咲屋姫神]] ） *神明神社　（[[アマテラス|天照大御神]]） == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} ==End Japanese== [[Category:岐阜県の神社]] [[Category:美濃国の式内社]] [[Category:岐阜県の旧郷社]] [[Category:養老町の歴史]] [[Category:養老町の建築物]]

神社|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''久久美雄彦神社'''（くくみおひこじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|岐阜県|Q131277}}{{jalink|養老郡|Q1155060}}{{jalink|養老町|Q153814}}にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}である。{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932|式内社}}の{{jalink|美濃国|Q864156}}{{jalink|多芸郡|Q11431050}}久久美雄彦神社であり、{{jalink|近代社格制度|Q712534|旧社格}}は{{jalink|郷社}}。久々美雄彦神社とも表記される。 {{jalink|養老山地|Q8062668}}の北端近くの中腹にあり、{{jalink|東海自然歩道|Q4249812}}沿いである。 == 沿革 == 創建時期は不明。{{jalink|仁明天皇|Q349271}}より{{jalink|承和 (日本)|Q1075605|承和}}5年（{{jalink|838年|Q23838}}）従五位下の神階を授けられたということが{{jalink|続日本後紀|Q1041301}}に記述されていることから、{{jalink|平安時代|Q193292}}初期以前の創建と推測される。 {{jalink|戦国時代 (日本)|Q204023|戦国時代}}の頃は久々見穗彦大明神、{{jalink|江戸時代|Q184963}}は久々美雄彦神社大明神と呼称されていた記述がある。{{jalink|明治|Q3125096}}6年（{{jalink|1873年|Q7757}}）に郷社となった際に久久美雄彦神社に改称している。 == 祭神 == *久久美雄彦神 :久久美雄彦神はどのような神かは不明。以下の説がある。 #{{jalink|伊富岐神社|Q11379321}}の多多美彦命と同一神。但し伊富岐神社の祭神も{{jalink|八岐大蛇}}、{{jalink|天火明命|Q10940411}}、{{jalink|ウガヤフキアエズ|Q1373935|草葦不合尊}}などの諸説あるのだが、多多美彦命（夷服岳神、気吹男神、伊富岐神ともいう）は{{jalink|伊吹山|Q2624360}}の神とされている。 #天戸辺尊の分神である久久美穂彦神。天戸辺尊の詳細は不明であるが、{{jalink|オオトノヂ・オオトノベ|Q11073597|大戸辺尊}}と推測される。 == 境内社 == *三社神社　（{{jalink|イザナギ|Q813858|伊邪那岐神}}・{{jalink|イザナミ|Q682306|伊邪那美神}}・{{jalink|コノハナノサクヤビメ|Q1781862|木花咲屋姫神}} ） *神明神社　（{{jalink|アマテラス||天照大御神}}） == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} ==End Japanese== [[Category:岐阜県の神社]] [[Category:美濃国の式内社]] [[Category:岐阜県の旧郷社]] [[Category:養老町の歴史]] [[Category:養老町の建築物]]

link|Q11368919}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称=久久美雄彦神社 |画像=[[File:Kukumiohiko shrine 2.jpg|250px]]
鳥居 |所在地={{jalink|岐阜県|Q131277}}{{jalink|養老郡|Q1155060}}{{jalink|養老町|Q153814}}沢田1341 |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神=久久美雄彦神 |社格={{jalink|式内社}}（小）論社、旧{{jalink|郷社|Q134917316}} |創建=不明 |本殿= |例祭=10月8日 |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Gifu|label=久久美雄彦
神社|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''久久美雄彦神社'''（くくみおひこじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|岐阜県|Q131277}}{{jalink|養老郡|Q1155060}}{{jalink|養老町|Q153814}}にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}である。{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932|式内社}}の{{jalink|美濃国|Q864156}}{{jalink|多芸郡|Q11431050}}久久美雄彦神社であり、{{jalink|近代社格制度|Q712534|旧社格}}は{{jalink|郷社|Q134917316}}。久々美雄彦神社とも表記される。 {{jalink|養老山地|Q8062668}}の北端近くの中腹にあり、{{jalink|東海自然歩道|Q4249812}}沿いである。 === 沿革 === 創建時期は不明。{{jalink|仁明天皇|Q349271}}より{{jalink|承和 (日本)|Q1075605|承和}}5年（{{jalink|838年|Q23838}}）従五位下の神階を授けられたということが{{jalink|続日本後紀|Q1041301}}に記述されていることから、{{jalink|平安時代|Q193292}}初期以前の創建と推測される。 {{jalink|戦国時代 (日本)|Q204023|戦国時代}}の頃は久々見穗彦大明神、{{jalink|江戸時代|Q184963}}は久々美雄彦神社大明神と呼称されていた記述がある。{{jalink|明治|Q3125096}}6年（{{jalink|1873年|Q7757}}）に郷社となった際に久久美雄彦神社に改称している。 === 祭神 === *久久美雄彦神 :久久美雄彦神はどのような神かは不明。以下の説がある。 #{{jalink|伊富岐神社|Q11379321}}の多多美彦命と同一神。但し伊富岐神社の祭神も{{jalink|八岐大蛇}}、{{jalink|天火明命|Q10940411}}、{{jalink|ウガヤフキアエズ|Q1373935|草葦不合尊}}などの諸説あるのだが、多多美彦命（夷服岳神、気吹男神、伊富岐神ともいう）は{{jalink|伊吹山|Q2624360}}の神とされている。 #天戸辺尊の分神である久久美穂彦神。天戸辺尊の詳細は不明であるが、{{jalink|オオトノヂ・オオトノベ|Q11073597|大戸辺尊}}と推測される。 === 境内社 === *三社神社　（{{jalink|イザナギ|Q813858|伊邪那岐神}}・{{jalink|イザナミ|Q682306|伊邪那美神}}・{{jalink|コノハナノサクヤビメ|Q1781862|木花咲屋姫神}} ） *神明神社　（{{jalink|アマテラス||天照大御神}}） === 脚注 === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:くくみおひこしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages]] [[Category:Pages]] [[Category:Autogenerated pages with jawiki interwikis, possibly accidentally overwritten]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mino Province]] [[Category:岐阜県の神社]] [[Category:美濃国の式内社]] [[Category:岐阜県の旧郷社]] [[Category:養老町の歴史]] [[Category:養老町の建築物]]

神社|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''久久美雄彦神社'''（くくみおひこじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|岐阜県|Q131277}}{{jalink|養老郡|Q1155060}}{{jalink|養老町|Q153814}}にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}である。{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932|式内社}}の{{jalink|美濃国|Q864156}}{{jalink|多芸郡|Q11431050}}久久美雄彦神社であり、{{jalink|近代社格制度|Q712534|旧社格}}は{{jalink|郷社|Q134917316}}。久々美雄彦神社とも表記される。 {{jalink|養老山地|Q8062668}}の北端近くの中腹にあり、{{jalink|東海自然歩道|Q4249812}}沿いである。 === 沿革 === 創建時期は不明。{{jalink|仁明天皇|Q349271}}より{{jalink|承和 (日本)|Q1075605|承和}}5年（{{jalink|838年|Q23838}}）従五位下の神階を授けられたということが{{jalink|続日本後紀|Q1041301}}に記述されていることから、{{jalink|平安時代|Q193292}}初期以前の創建と推測される。 {{jalink|戦国時代 (日本)|Q204023|戦国時代}}の頃は久々見穗彦大明神、{{jalink|江戸時代|Q184963}}は久々美雄彦神社大明神と呼称されていた記述がある。{{jalink|明治|Q3125096}}6年（{{jalink|1873年|Q7757}}）に郷社となった際に久久美雄彦神社に改称している。 === 祭神 === *久久美雄彦神 :久久美雄彦神はどのような神かは不明。以下の説がある。 #{{jalink|伊富岐神社|Q11379321}}の多多美彦命と同一神。但し伊富岐神社の祭神も{{jalink|八岐大蛇}}、{{jalink|天火明命|Q10940411}}、{{jalink|ウガヤフキアエズ|Q1373935|草葦不合尊}}などの諸説あるのだが、多多美彦命（夷服岳神、気吹男神、伊富岐神ともいう）は{{jalink|伊吹山|Q2624360}}の神とされている。 #天戸辺尊の分神である久久美穂彦神。天戸辺尊の詳細は不明であるが、{{jalink|オオトノヂ・オオトノベ|Q11073597|大戸辺尊}}と推測される。 === 境内社 === *三社神社　（{{jalink|イザナギ|Q813858|伊邪那岐神}}・{{jalink|イザナミ|Q682306|伊邪那美神}}・{{jalink|コノハナノサクヤビメ|Q1781862|木花咲屋姫神}} ） *神明神社　（{{jalink|アマテラス||天照大御神}}） === 脚注 === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:くくみおひこしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages]] [[Category:Pages]] [[Category:Autogenerated pages with jawiki interwikis, possibly accidentally overwritten]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mino Province]] [[Category:岐阜県の神社]] [[Category:美濃国の式内社]] [[Category:岐阜県の旧郷社]] [[Category:養老町の歴史]] [[Category:養老町の建築物]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:need translation]]

神社|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''久久美雄彦神社'''（くくみおひこじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|岐阜県|Q131277}}{{jalink|養老郡|Q1155060}}{{jalink|養老町|Q153814}}にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}である。{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932|式内社}}の{{jalink|美濃国|Q864156}}{{jalink|多芸郡|Q11431050}}久久美雄彦神社であり、{{jalink|近代社格制度|Q712534|旧社格}}は{{jalink|郷社|Q134917316}}。久々美雄彦神社とも表記される。 {{jalink|養老山地|Q8062668}}の北端近くの中腹にあり、{{jalink|東海自然歩道|Q4249812}}沿いである。 === 沿革 === 創建時期は不明。{{jalink|仁明天皇|Q349271}}より{{jalink|承和 (日本)|Q1075605|承和}}5年（{{jalink|838年|Q23838}}）従五位下の神階を授けられたということが{{jalink|続日本後紀|Q1041301}}に記述されていることから、{{jalink|平安時代|Q193292}}初期以前の創建と推測される。 {{jalink|戦国時代 (日本)|Q204023|戦国時代}}の頃は久々見穗彦大明神、{{jalink|江戸時代|Q184963}}は久々美雄彦神社大明神と呼称されていた記述がある。{{jalink|明治|Q3125096}}6年（{{jalink|1873年|Q7757}}）に郷社となった際に久久美雄彦神社に改称している。 === 祭神 === *久久美雄彦神 :久久美雄彦神はどのような神かは不明。以下の説がある。 #{{jalink|伊富岐神社|Q11379321}}の多多美彦命と同一神。但し伊富岐神社の祭神も{{jalink|八岐大蛇}}、{{jalink|天火明命|Q10940411}}、{{jalink|ウガヤフキアエズ|Q1373935|草葦不合尊}}などの諸説あるのだが、多多美彦命（夷服岳神、気吹男神、伊富岐神ともいう）は{{jalink|伊吹山|Q2624360}}の神とされている。 #天戸辺尊の分神である久久美穂彦神。天戸辺尊の詳細は不明であるが、{{jalink|オオトノヂ・オオトノベ|Q11073597|大戸辺尊}}と推測される。 === 境内社 === *三社神社　（{{jalink|イザナギ|Q813858|伊邪那岐神}}・{{jalink|イザナミ|Q682306|伊邪那美神}}・{{jalink|コノハナノサクヤビメ|Q1781862|木花咲屋姫神}} ） *神明神社　（{{jalink|アマテラス||天照大御神}}） === 脚注 === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:くくみおひこしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kukumiohiko Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mino Province]] [[Category:岐阜県の神社]] [[Category:美濃国の式内社]] [[Category:岐阜県の旧郷社]] [[Category:養老町の歴史]] [[Category:養老町の建築物]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:need translation]]

神社|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''久久美雄彦神社'''（くくみおひこじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|岐阜県|Q131277}}{{jalink|養老郡|Q1155060}}{{jalink|養老町|Q153814}}にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}である。{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932|式内社}}の{{jalink|美濃国|Q864156}}{{jalink|多芸郡|Q11431050}}久久美雄彦神社であり、{{jalink|近代社格制度|Q712534|旧社格}}は{{jalink|郷社|Q134917316}}。久々美雄彦神社とも表記される。 {{jalink|養老山地|Q8062668}}の北端近くの中腹にあり、{{jalink|東海自然歩道|Q4249812}}沿いである。 === 沿革 === 創建時期は不明。{{jalink|仁明天皇|Q349271}}より{{jalink|承和 (日本)|Q1075605|承和}}5年（{{jalink|838年|Q23838}}）従五位下の神階を授けられたということが{{jalink|続日本後紀|Q1041301}}に記述されていることから、{{jalink|平安時代|Q193292}}初期以前の創建と推測される。 {{jalink|戦国時代 (日本)|Q204023|戦国時代}}の頃は久々見穗彦大明神、{{jalink|江戸時代|Q184963}}は久々美雄彦神社大明神と呼称されていた記述がある。{{jalink|明治|Q3125096}}6年（{{jalink|1873年|Q7757}}）に郷社となった際に久久美雄彦神社に改称している。 === 祭神 === *久久美雄彦神 :久久美雄彦神はどのような神かは不明。以下の説がある。 #{{jalink|伊富岐神社|Q11379321}}の多多美彦命と同一神。但し伊富岐神社の祭神も{{jalink|八岐大蛇}}、{{jalink|天火明命|Q10940411}}、{{jalink|ウガヤフキアエズ|Q1373935|草葦不合尊}}などの諸説あるのだが、多多美彦命（夷服岳神、気吹男神、伊富岐神ともいう）は{{jalink|伊吹山|Q2624360}}の神とされている。 #天戸辺尊の分神である久久美穂彦神。天戸辺尊の詳細は不明であるが、{{jalink|オオトノヂ・オオトノベ|Q11073597|大戸辺尊}}と推測される。 === 境内社 === *三社神社　（{{jalink|イザナギ|Q813858|伊邪那岐神}}・{{jalink|イザナミ|Q682306|伊邪那美神}}・{{jalink|コノハナノサクヤビメ|Q1781862|木花咲屋姫神}} ） *神明神社　（{{jalink|アマテラス||天照大御神}}） === 脚注 === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:くくみおひこしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kukumiohiko Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kukumiohiko Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Kukumiohiko Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mino Province]] [[Category:岐阜県の神社]] [[Category:美濃国の式内社]] [[Category:岐阜県の旧郷社]] [[Category:養老町の歴史]] [[Category:養老町の建築物]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:need translation]]

It is located in [[Mino Province]]. == instance of (P31) == * {{ill|Shinto shrine|lt=Shinto shrine|ar|جينجا|arz|ضريح شنتو|bcl|Shinto shrine|ca|Jinja|cs|Šintoistická link|Q11368919}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称=Kukumiohiko Shrine |画像=[[File:Kukumiohiko shrine 2.jpg|250px]]
Torii |所在地={{jalink|Gifu Prefecture|Q131277}}{{jalink|Yōrō District|Q1155060}}{{jalink|Yōrō|Q153814}} Sawada 1341 |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神=Kukumiohiko-no-Kami |社格={{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (small) candidate shrine, former {{jalink|gōsha|Q134917316}} |創建=Unknown |本殿= |例祭=October 8 |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Gifu|label=Kukumiohiko
Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Kukumiohiko Shrine''' (くくみおひこじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in {{jalink|Yōrō|Q153814}}, {{jalink|Yōrō District|Q1155060}}, {{jalink|Gifu Prefecture|Q131277}}. It is the Kukumiohiko Shrine of {{jalink|Tagi District|Q11431050}} in {{jalink|Mino Province|Q864156}} listed in the {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|Engishiki}}, and its former rank under the {{jalink|modern shrine ranking system|Q712534}} was {{jalink|gōsha|Q134917316}}. It is also written as Kugumiohiko Shrine. It stands along the {{jalink|Tōkai Nature Trail|Q4249812}}, on the mid-slope near the northern end of the {{jalink|Yōrō Mountains|Q8062668}}. === History === The founding period is unknown. Because the ''{{jalink|Shoku Nihon Kōki|Q1041301}}'' records that it received the divine rank of Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade in {{jalink|Jōwa (Japan)|Q1075605|Jōwa}} 5 ({{jalink|838|Q23838}}) from {{jalink|Emperor Ninmyō|Q349271}}, it is presumed to have been founded no later than the early {{jalink|Heian period|Q193292}}. There are records that during the {{jalink|Sengoku period (Japan)|Q204023|Sengoku period}} it was called Kugumihohiko Daimyōjin, and in the {{jalink|Edo period|Q184963}} it was called Kugumiohiko Jinja Daimyōjin. When it became a gōsha in {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}} 6 ({{jalink|1873|Q7757}}), it was renamed Kukumiohiko Shrine. === Enshrined deity === *Kukumiohiko-no-Kami :What kind of deity Kukumiohiko-no-Kami is remains unclear. The following theories exist: #The same deity as Tatamihiko-no-Mikoto of {{jalink|Ibuki Shrine|Q11379321}}. However, deities of Ibuki Shrine also have multiple theories, including {{jalink|Yamata no Orochi}}, {{jalink|Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto|Q10940411}}, and {{jalink|Ugayafukiaezu|Q1373935|Kusayafugō-no-Mikoto}}; Tatamihiko-no-Mikoto (also called Ebisudake-no-Kami, Kibuki-no-O-no-Kami, or Ibuki-no-Kami) is regarded as the deity of {{jalink|Mount Ibuki|Q2624360}}. #Kukumihohiko-no-Kami, a divided deity of Ama-no-Tobeno-Mikoto. Details of Ama-no-Tobeno-Mikoto are unknown, but it is presumed to be {{jalink|Ōtonoji and Ōtonobe|Q11073597|Ōtobe-no-Mikoto}}. === Precinct shrines === *Sansha Shrine ({{jalink|Izanagi|Q813858|Izanagi-no-Kami}}, {{jalink|Izanami|Q682306|Izanami-no-Kami}}, {{jalink|Konohanasakuyahime|Q1781862|Konohanasakuya-hime-no-Kami}}) *Shinmei Shrine ({{jalink|Amaterasu||Amaterasu Ōmikami}}) === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:くくみおひこしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kukumiohiko Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kukumiohiko Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Kukumiohiko Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mino Province]] [[Category:岐阜県の神社]] [[Category:美濃国の式内社]] [[Category:岐阜県の旧郷社]] [[Category:養老町の歴史]] [[Category:養老町の建築物]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:need translation]]

Details of Ama-no-Tobeno-Mikoto are unknown, but it is presumed to be {{jalink|Ōtonoji and Ōtonobe|Q11073597|Ōtobe-no-Mikoto}}. === Precinct shrines === *Sansha Shrine ({{jalink|Izanagi|Q813858|Izanagi-no-Kami}}, {{jalink|Izanami|Q682306|Izanami-no-Kami}}, {{jalink|Konohanasakuyahime|Q1781862|Konohanasakuya-hime-no-Kami}}) *Shinmei Shrine ({{jalink|Amaterasu||Amaterasu Ōmikami}}) === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:くくみおひこしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kukumiohiko Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kukumiohiko Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Kukumiohiko Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mino Province]] [[Category:岐阜県の神社]] [[Category:美濃国の式内社]] [[Category:岐阜県の旧郷社]] [[Category:養老町の歴史]] [[Category:養老町の建築物]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:need translation]]

link|Q11368919}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称=Kukumiohiko Shrine |画像=[[File:Kukumiohiko shrine 2.jpg|250px]]
Torii |所在地={{jalink|Gifu Prefecture|Q131277}}{{jalink|Yōrō District|Q1155060}}{{jalink|Yōrō|Q153814}} Sawada 1341 |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神=Kukumiohiko-no-Kami |社格={{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (small) candidate shrine, former {{jalink|gōsha|Q134917316}} |創建=Unknown |本殿= |例祭=October 8 |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Gifu|label=Kukumiohiko
Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Kukumiohiko Shrine''' (くくみおひこじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in {{jalink|Yōrō|Q153814}}, {{jalink|Yōrō District|Q1155060}}, {{jalink|Gifu Prefecture|Q131277}}. It is the Kukumiohiko Shrine of {{jalink|Tagi District|Q11431050}} in {{jalink|Mino Province|Q864156}} listed in the {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|Engishiki}}, and its former rank under the {{jalink|modern shrine ranking system|Q712534}} was {{jalink|gōsha|Q134917316}}. It is also written as Kugumiohiko Shrine. It stands along the {{jalink|Tōkai Nature Trail|Q4249812}}, on the mid-slope near the northern end of the {{jalink|Yōrō Mountains|Q8062668}}. === History === The founding period is unknown. Because the ''{{jalink|Shoku Nihon Kōki|Q1041301}}'' records that it received the divine rank of Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade in {{jalink|Jōwa (Japan)|Q1075605|Jōwa}} 5 ({{jalink|838|Q23838}}) from {{jalink|Emperor Ninmyō|Q349271}}, it is presumed to have been founded no later than the early {{jalink|Heian period|Q193292}}. There are records that during the {{jalink|Sengoku period (Japan)|Q204023|Sengoku period}} it was called Kugumihohiko Daimyōjin, and in the {{jalink|Edo period|Q184963}} it was called Kugumiohiko Jinja Daimyōjin. When it became a gōsha in {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}} 6 ({{jalink|1873|Q7757}}), it was renamed Kukumiohiko Shrine. === Enshrined deity === *Kukumiohiko-no-Kami :What kind of deity Kukumiohiko-no-Kami is remains unclear. The following theories exist: #The same deity as Tatamihiko-no-Mikoto of {{jalink|Ibuki Shrine|Q11379321}}. However, deities of Ibuki Shrine also have multiple theories, including {{jalink|Yamata no Orochi}}, {{jalink|Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto|Q10940411}}, and {{jalink|Ugayafukiaezu|Q1373935|Kusayafugō-no-Mikoto}}; Tatamihiko-no-Mikoto (also called Ebisudake-no-Kami, Kibuki-no-O-no-Kami, or Ibuki-no-Kami) is regarded as the deity of {{jalink|Mount Ibuki|Q2624360}}. #Kukumihohiko-no-Kami, a divided deity of Ama-no-Tobeno-Mikoto. Details of Ama-no-Tobeno-Mikoto are unknown, but it is presumed to be {{jalink|Ōtonoji and Ōtonobe|Q11073597|Ōtobe-no-Mikoto}}. === Precinct shrines === *Sansha Shrine ({{jalink|Izanagi|Q813858|Izanagi-no-Kami}}, {{jalink|Izanami|Q682306|Izanami-no-Kami}}, {{jalink|Konohanasakuyahime|Q1781862|Konohanasakuya-hime-no-Kami}}) *Shinmei Shrine ({{jalink|Amaterasu||Amaterasu Ōmikami}}) === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:くくみおひこしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kukumiohiko Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kukumiohiko Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Kukumiohiko Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mino Province]] [[Category:岐阜県の神社]] [[Category:美濃国の式内社]] [[Category:岐阜県の旧郷社]] [[Category:養老町の歴史]] [[Category:養老町の建築物]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:need translation]]

link|Q11368919}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称=Kukumiohiko Shrine |画像=[[File:Kukumiohiko shrine 2.jpg|250px]]
Torii |所在地={{jalink|Gifu Prefecture|Q131277}}{{jalink|Yōrō District|Q1155060}}{{jalink|Yōrō|Q153814}} Sawada 1341 |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神=Kukumiohiko-no-Kami |社格={{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (small) candidate shrine, former {{jalink|gōsha|Q134917316}} |創建=Unknown |本殿= |例祭=October 8 |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Gifu|label=Kukumiohiko
Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Kukumiohiko Shrine''' (くくみおひこじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in {{jalink|Yōrō|Q153814}}, {{jalink|Yōrō District|Q1155060}}, {{jalink|Gifu Prefecture|Q131277}}. It is the Kukumiohiko Shrine of {{jalink|Tagi District|Q11431050}} in {{jalink|Mino Province|Q864156}} listed in the {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|Engishiki}}, and its former rank under the {{jalink|modern shrine ranking system|Q712534}} was {{jalink|gōsha|Q134917316}}. It is also written as Kugumiohiko Shrine. It stands along the {{jalink|Tōkai Nature Trail|Q4249812}}, on the mid-slope near the northern end of the {{jalink|Yōrō Mountains|Q8062668}}. === History === The founding period is unknown. Because the ''{{jalink|Shoku Nihon Kōki|Q1041301}}'' records that it received the divine rank of Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade in {{jalink|Jōwa (Japan)|Q1075605|Jōwa}} 5 ({{jalink|838|Q23838}}) from {{jalink|Emperor Ninmyō|Q349271}}, it is presumed to have been founded no later than the early {{jalink|Heian period|Q193292}}. There are records that during the {{jalink|Sengoku period (Japan)|Q204023|Sengoku period}} it was called Kugumihohiko Daimyōjin, and in the {{jalink|Edo period|Q184963}} it was called Kugumiohiko Jinja Daimyōjin. When it became a gōsha in {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}} 6 ({{jalink|1873|Q7757}}), it was renamed Kukumiohiko Shrine. === Enshrined deity === *Kukumiohiko-no-Kami :What kind of deity Kukumiohiko-no-Kami is remains unclear. The following theories exist: #The same deity as Tatamihiko-no-Mikoto of {{jalink|Ibuki Shrine|Q11379321}}. However, deities of Ibuki Shrine also have multiple theories, including {{jalink|Yamata no Orochi}}, {{jalink|Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto|Q10940411}}, and {{jalink|Ugayafukiaezu|Q1373935|Kusayafugō-no-Mikoto}}; Tatamihiko-no-Mikoto (also called Ebisudake-no-Kami, Kibuki-no-O-no-Kami, or Ibuki-no-Kami) is regarded as the deity of {{jalink|Mount Ibuki|Q2624360}}. #Kukumihohiko-no-Kami, a divided deity of Ama-no-Tobeno-Mikoto. Details of Ama-no-Tobeno-Mikoto are unknown, but it is presumed to be {{jalink|Ōtonoji and Ōtonobe|Q11073597|Ōtobe-no-Mikoto}}. === Precinct shrines === *Sansha Shrine ({{jalink|Izanagi|Q813858|Izanagi-no-Kami}}, {{jalink|Izanami|Q682306|Izanami-no-Kami}}, {{jalink|Konohanasakuyahime|Q1781862|Konohanasakuya-hime-no-Kami}}) *Shinmei Shrine ({{jalink|Amaterasu||Amaterasu Ōmikami}}) === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:くくみおひこしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kukumiohiko Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kukumiohiko Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Kukumiohiko Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mino Province]] [[Category:岐阜県の神社]] [[Category:美濃国の式内社]] [[Category:岐阜県の旧郷社]] [[Category:養老町の歴史]] [[Category:養老町の建築物]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:need translation]]

It is the Kukumiohiko Shrine of {{jalink|Tagi District|Q11431050}} in {{jalink|Mino Province|Q864156}} listed in the {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|Engishiki}}, and its former rank under the {{jalink|modern shrine ranking system|Q712534}} was {{jalink|gōsha|Q134917316}}. It is also written as Kugumiohiko Shrine. It stands along the {{jalink|Tōkai Nature Trail|Q4249812}}, on the mid-slope near the northern end of the {{jalink|Yōrō Mountains|Q8062668}}. === History === The founding period is unknown. Because the ''{{jalink|Shoku Nihon Kōki|Q1041301}}'' records that it received the divine rank of Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade in {{jalink|Jōwa (Japan)|Q1075605|Jōwa}} 5 ({{jalink|838|Q23838}}) from {{jalink|Emperor Ninmyō|Q349271}}, it is presumed to have been founded no later than the early {{jalink|Heian period|Q193292}}. There are records that during the {{jalink|Sengoku period (Japan)|Q204023|Sengoku period}} it was called Kugumihohiko Daimyōjin, and in the {{jalink|Edo period|Q184963}} it was called Kugumiohiko Jinja Daimyōjin. When it became a gōsha in {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}} 6 ({{jalink|1873|Q7757}}), it was renamed Kukumiohiko Shrine. === Enshrined deity === *Kukumiohiko-no-Kami :What kind of deity Kukumiohiko-no-Kami is remains unclear. The following theories exist: #The same deity as Tatamihiko-no-Mikoto of {{jalink|Ibuki Shrine|Q11379321}}. However, deities of Ibuki Shrine also have multiple theories, including {{jalink|Yamata no Orochi}}, {{jalink|Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto|Q10940411}}, and {{jalink|Ugayafukiaezu|Q1373935|Kusayafugō-no-Mikoto}}; Tatamihiko-no-Mikoto (also called Ebisudake-no-Kami, Kibuki-no-O-no-Kami, or Ibuki-no-Kami) is regarded as the deity of {{jalink|Mount Ibuki|Q2624360}}. #Kukumihohiko-no-Kami, a divided deity of Ama-no-Tobeno-Mikoto. Details of Ama-no-Tobeno-Mikoto are unknown, but it is presumed to be {{jalink|Ōtonoji and Ōtonobe|Q11073597|Ōtobe-no-Mikoto}}. === Precinct shrines === *Sansha Shrine ({{jalink|Izanagi|Q813858|Izanagi-no-Kami}}, {{jalink|Izanami|Q682306|Izanami-no-Kami}}, {{jalink|Konohanasakuyahime|Q1781862|Konohanasakuya-hime-no-Kami}}) *Shinmei Shrine ({{jalink|Amaterasu||Amaterasu Ōmikami}}) === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:くくみおひこしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kukumiohiko Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kukumiohiko Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Kukumiohiko Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mino Province]] [[Category:岐阜県の神社]] [[Category:美濃国の式内社]] [[Category:岐阜県の旧郷社]] [[Category:養老町の歴史]] [[Category:養老町の建築物]] [[Category:pages]]

link|Q11368919}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称=Kukumiohiko Shrine |画像=[[File:Kukumiohiko shrine 2.jpg|250px]]
Torii |所在地={{jalink|Gifu Prefecture|Q131277}}{{jalink|Yōrō District|Q1155060}}{{jalink|Yōrō|Q153814}} Sawada 1341 |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神=Kukumiohiko-no-Kami |社格={{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (small) candidate shrine, former {{jalink|gōsha|Q134917316}} |創建=Unknown |本殿= |例祭=October 8 |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Gifu|label=Kukumiohiko
Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Kukumiohiko Shrine''' (くくみおひこじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in {{jalink|Yōrō|Q153814}}, {{jalink|Yōrō District|Q1155060}}, {{jalink|Gifu Prefecture|Q131277}}. It is the Kukumiohiko Shrine of {{jalink|Tagi District|Q11431050}} in {{jalink|Mino Province|Q864156}} listed in the {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|Engishiki}}, and its former rank under the {{jalink|modern shrine ranking system|Q712534}} was {{jalink|gōsha|Q134917316}}. It is also written as Kugumiohiko Shrine. It stands along the {{jalink|Tōkai Nature Trail|Q4249812}}, on the mid-slope near the northern end of the {{jalink|Yōrō Mountains|Q8062668}}. === History === The founding period is unknown. Because the ''{{jalink|Shoku Nihon Kōki|Q1041301}}'' records that it received the divine rank of Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade in {{jalink|Jōwa (Japan)|Q1075605|Jōwa}} 5 ({{jalink|838|Q23838}}) from {{jalink|Emperor Ninmyō|Q349271}}, it is presumed to have been founded no later than the early {{jalink|Heian period|Q193292}}. There are records that during the {{jalink|Sengoku period (Japan)|Q204023|Sengoku period}} it was called Kugumihohiko Daimyōjin, and in the {{jalink|Edo period|Q184963}} it was called Kugumiohiko Jinja Daimyōjin. When it became a gōsha in {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}} 6 ({{jalink|1873|Q7757}}), it was renamed Kukumiohiko Shrine. === Enshrined deity === *Kukumiohiko-no-Kami :What kind of deity Kukumiohiko-no-Kami is remains unclear. The following theories exist: #The same deity as Tatamihiko-no-Mikoto of {{jalink|Ibuki Shrine|Q11379321}}. However, deities of Ibuki Shrine also have multiple theories, including {{jalink|Yamata no Orochi}}, {{jalink|Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto|Q10940411}}, and {{jalink|Ugayafukiaezu|Q1373935|Kusayafugō-no-Mikoto}}; Tatamihiko-no-Mikoto (also called Ebisudake-no-Kami, Kibuki-no-O-no-Kami, or Ibuki-no-Kami) is regarded as the deity of {{jalink|Mount Ibuki|Q2624360}}. #Kukumihohiko-no-Kami, a divided deity of Ama-no-Tobeno-Mikoto. Details of Ama-no-Tobeno-Mikoto are unknown, but it is presumed to be {{jalink|Ōtonoji and Ōtonobe|Q11073597|Ōtobe-no-Mikoto}}. === Precinct shrines === *Sansha Shrine ({{jalink|Izanagi|Q813858|Izanagi-no-Kami}}, {{jalink|Izanami|Q682306|Izanami-no-Kami}}, {{jalink|Konohanasakuyahime|Q1781862|Konohanasakuya-hime-no-Kami}}) *Shinmei Shrine ({{jalink|Amaterasu||Amaterasu Ōmikami}}) === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:くくみおひこしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kukumiohiko Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kukumiohiko Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Kukumiohiko Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mino Province]] [[Category:岐阜県の神社]] [[Category:美濃国の式内社]] [[Category:岐阜県の旧郷社]] [[Category:養老町の歴史]] [[Category:養老町の建築物]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:qqq]]

Torii |所在地={{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277}}{{ill|lt=Yōrō District|Q1155060|WD=Q1155060}}{{ill|lt=Yōrō|Q153814|WD=Q153814}} Sawada 1341 |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神=Kukumiohiko-no-Kami |社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}} (small) candidate shrine, former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}} |創建=Unknown |本殿= |例祭=October 8 |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Gifu|label=Kukumiohiko
Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Kukumiohiko Shrine''' (くくみおひこじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in {{ill|lt=Yōrō|Q153814|WD=Q153814}}, {{ill|lt=Yōrō District|Q1155060|WD=Q1155060}}, {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277}}. It is the Kukumiohiko Shrine of {{ill|lt=Tagi District|Q11431050|WD=Q11431050}} in {{ill|lt=Mino Province|Q864156|WD=Q864156}} listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Engishiki}}, and its former rank under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534}} was {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}}. It is also written as Kugumiohiko Shrine. It stands along the {{ill|lt=Tōkai Nature Trail|Q4249812|WD=Q4249812}}, on the mid-slope near the northern end of the {{ill|lt=Yōrō Mountains|Q8062668|WD=Q8062668}}. === History === The founding period is unknown. Because the ''{{ill|lt=Shoku Nihon Kōki|Q1041301|WD=Q1041301}}'' records that it received the divine rank of Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade in {{ill|lt=Jōwa (Japan)|Q1075605|WD=Q1075605|lt=Jōwa}} 5 ({{ill|lt=838|Q23838|WD=Q23838}}) from {{ill|lt=Emperor Ninmyō|Q349271|WD=Q349271}}, it is presumed to have been founded no later than the early {{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|WD=Q193292}}. There are records that during the {{ill|lt=Sengoku period (Japan)|Q204023|WD=Q204023|lt=Sengoku period}} it was called Kugumihohiko Daimyōjin, and in the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963}} it was called Kugumiohiko Jinja Daimyōjin. When it became a gōsha in {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} 6 ({{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757}}), it was renamed Kukumiohiko Shrine. === Enshrined deity === *Kukumiohiko-no-Kami :What kind of deity Kukumiohiko-no-Kami is remains unclear. The following theories exist: #The same deity as Tatamihiko-no-Mikoto of {{ill|lt=Ibuki Shrine|Q11379321|WD=Q11379321}}. However, deities of Ibuki Shrine also have multiple theories, including {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yamata no Orochi|UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto|Q10940411|WD=Q10940411}}, and {{ill|lt=Ugayafukiaezu|Q1373935|WD=Q1373935|lt=Kusayafugō-no-Mikoto}}; Tatamihiko-no-Mikoto (also called Ebisudake-no-Kami, Kibuki-no-O-no-Kami, or Ibuki-no-Kami) is regarded as the deity of {{ill|lt=Mount Ibuki|Q2624360|WD=Q2624360}}. #Kukumihohiko-no-Kami, a divided deity of Ama-no-Tobeno-Mikoto. Details of Ama-no-Tobeno-Mikoto are unknown, but it is presumed to be {{ill|lt=Ōtonoji and Ōtonobe|Q11073597|WD=Q11073597|lt=Ōtobe-no-Mikoto}}. === Precinct shrines === *Sansha Shrine ({{ill|lt=Izanagi|Q813858|WD=Q813858|lt=Izanagi-no-Kami}}, {{ill|lt=Izanami|Q682306|WD=Q682306|lt=Izanami-no-Kami}}, {{ill|lt=Konohanasakuyahime|Q1781862|WD=Q1781862|lt=Konohanasakuya-hime-no-Kami}}) *Shinmei Shrine ({{ill|lt=Amaterasu||WD=|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami}}) === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:くくみおひこしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kukumiohiko Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kukumiohiko Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Kukumiohiko Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mino Province]] [[Category:岐阜県の神社]] [[Category:美濃国の式内社]] [[Category:岐阜県の旧郷社]] [[Category:養老町の歴史]] [[Category:養老町の建築物]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:qqq]]

Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Kukumiohiko Shrine''' (くくみおひこじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in {{ill|lt=Yōrō|Q153814|WD=Q153814}}, {{ill|lt=Yōrō District|Q1155060|WD=Q1155060}}, {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277}}. It is the Kukumiohiko Shrine of {{ill|lt=Tagi District|Q11431050|WD=Q11431050}} in {{ill|lt=Mino Province|Q864156|WD=Q864156}} listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Engishiki}}, and its former rank under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534}} was {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}}. It is also written as Kugumiohiko Shrine. It stands along the {{ill|lt=Tōkai Nature Trail|Q4249812|WD=Q4249812}}, on the mid-slope near the northern end of the {{ill|lt=Yōrō Mountains|Q8062668|WD=Q8062668}}. === History === The founding period is unknown. Because the ''{{ill|lt=Shoku Nihon Kōki|Q1041301|WD=Q1041301}}'' records that it received the divine rank of Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade in {{ill|lt=Jōwa (Japan)|Q1075605|WD=Q1075605|lt=Jōwa}} 5 ({{ill|lt=838|Q23838|WD=Q23838}}) from {{ill|lt=Emperor Ninmyō|Q349271|WD=Q349271}}, it is presumed to have been founded no later than the early {{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|WD=Q193292}}. There are records that during the {{ill|lt=Sengoku period (Japan)|Q204023|WD=Q204023|lt=Sengoku period}} it was called Kugumihohiko Daimyōjin, and in the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963}} it was called Kugumiohiko Jinja Daimyōjin. When it became a gōsha in {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} 6 ({{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757}}), it was renamed Kukumiohiko Shrine. === Enshrined deity === *Kukumiohiko-no-Kami :What kind of deity Kukumiohiko-no-Kami is remains unclear. The following theories exist: #The same deity as Tatamihiko-no-Mikoto of {{ill|lt=Ibuki Shrine|Q11379321|WD=Q11379321}}. However, deities of Ibuki Shrine also have multiple theories, including {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yamata no Orochi|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto|Q10940411|WD=Q10940411}}, and {{ill|lt=Ugayafukiaezu|Q1373935|WD=Q1373935|lt=Kusayafugō-no-Mikoto}}; Tatamihiko-no-Mikoto (also called Ebisudake-no-Kami, Kibuki-no-O-no-Kami, or Ibuki-no-Kami) is regarded as the deity of {{ill|lt=Mount Ibuki|Q2624360|WD=Q2624360}}. #Kukumihohiko-no-Kami, a divided deity of Ama-no-Tobeno-Mikoto. Details of Ama-no-Tobeno-Mikoto are unknown, but it is presumed to be {{ill|lt=Ōtonoji and Ōtonobe|Q11073597|WD=Q11073597|lt=Ōtobe-no-Mikoto}}. === Precinct shrines === *Sansha Shrine ({{ill|lt=Izanagi|Q813858|WD=Q813858|lt=Izanagi-no-Kami}}, {{ill|lt=Izanami|Q682306|WD=Q682306|lt=Izanami-no-Kami}}, {{ill|lt=Konohanasakuyahime|Q1781862|WD=Q1781862|lt=Konohanasakuya-hime-no-Kami}}) *Shinmei Shrine ({{ill|lt=Amaterasu|UNKNOWN|WD=|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami}}) === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:くくみおひこしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kukumiohiko Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kukumiohiko Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Kukumiohiko Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mino Province]] [[Category:岐阜県の神社]] [[Category:美濃国の式内社]] [[Category:岐阜県の旧郷社]] [[Category:養老町の歴史]] [[Category:養老町の建築物]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:qqq]]

The following theories exist: #The same deity as Tatamihiko-no-Mikoto of {{ill|lt=Ibuki Shrine|Q11379321|WD=Q11379321}}. However, deities of Ibuki Shrine also have multiple theories, including {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yamata no Orochi|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto|Q10940411|WD=Q10940411}}, and {{ill|lt=Ugayafukiaezu|Q1373935|WD=Q1373935|lt=Kusayafugō-no-Mikoto}}; Tatamihiko-no-Mikoto (also called Ebisudake-no-Kami, Kibuki-no-O-no-Kami, or Ibuki-no-Kami) is regarded as the deity of {{ill|lt=Mount Ibuki|Q2624360|WD=Q2624360}}. #Kukumihohiko-no-Kami, a divided deity of Ama-no-Tobeno-Mikoto. Details of Ama-no-Tobeno-Mikoto are unknown, but it is presumed to be {{ill|lt=Ōtonoji and Ōtonobe|Q11073597|WD=Q11073597|lt=Ōtobe-no-Mikoto}}. === Precinct shrines === *Sansha Shrine ({{ill|lt=Izanagi|Q813858|WD=Q813858|lt=Izanagi-no-Kami}}, {{ill|lt=Izanami|Q682306|WD=Q682306|lt=Izanami-no-Kami}}, {{ill|lt=Konohanasakuyahime|Q1781862|WD=Q1781862|lt=Konohanasakuya-hime-no-Kami}}) *Shinmei Shrine ({{ill|lt=Amaterasu|UNKNOWN|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami}}) === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:くくみおひこしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kukumiohiko Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kukumiohiko Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Kukumiohiko Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mino Province]] [[Category:岐阜県の神社]] [[Category:美濃国の式内社]] [[Category:岐阜県の旧郷社]] [[Category:養老町の歴史]] [[Category:養老町の建築物]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:qqq]]

It is the Kukumiohiko Shrine of {{ill|lt=Tagi District|Q11431050|WD=Q11431050}} in {{ill|lt=Mino Province|Q864156|WD=Q864156}} listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Engishiki}}, and its former rank under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534}} was {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}}. It is also written as Kugumiohiko Shrine. It stands along the {{ill|lt=Tōkai Nature Trail|Q4249812|WD=Q4249812}}, on the mid-slope near the northern end of the {{ill|lt=Yōrō Mountains|Q8062668|WD=Q8062668}}. === History === The founding period is unknown. Because the ''{{ill|lt=Shoku Nihon Kōki|Q1041301|WD=Q1041301}}'' records that it received the divine rank of Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade in {{ill|lt=Jōwa (Japan)|Q1075605|WD=Q1075605|lt=Jōwa}} 5 ({{ill|lt=838|Q23838|WD=Q23838}}) from {{ill|lt=Emperor Ninmyō|Q349271|WD=Q349271}}, it is presumed to have been founded no later than the early {{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|WD=Q193292}}. There are records that during the {{ill|lt=Sengoku period (Japan)|Q204023|WD=Q204023|lt=Sengoku period}} it was called Kugumihohiko Daimyōjin, and in the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963}} it was called Kugumiohiko Jinja Daimyōjin. When it became a gōsha in {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} 6 ({{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757}}), it was renamed Kukumiohiko Shrine. === Enshrined deity === *Kukumiohiko-no-Kami :What kind of deity Kukumiohiko-no-Kami is remains unclear. However, deities of Ibuki Shrine also have multiple theories, including {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yamata no Orochi|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto|Q10940411|WD=Q10940411}}, and {{ill|lt=Ugayafukiaezu|Q1373935|WD=Q1373935|lt=Kusayafugō-no-Mikoto}}; Tatamihiko-no-Mikoto (also called Ebisudake-no-Kami, Kibuki-no-O-no-Kami, or Ibuki-no-Kami) is regarded as the deity of {{ill|lt=Mount Ibuki|Q2624360|WD=Q2624360}}. #Kukumihohiko-no-Kami, a divided deity of Ama-no-Tobeno-Mikoto. Details of Ama-no-Tobeno-Mikoto are unknown, but it is presumed to be {{ill|lt=Ōtonoji and Ōtonobe|Q11073597|WD=Q11073597|lt=Ōtobe-no-Mikoto}}. === Precinct shrines === *Sansha Shrine ({{ill|lt=Izanagi|Q813858|WD=Q813858|lt=Izanagi-no-Kami}}, {{ill|lt=Izanami|Q682306|WD=Q682306|lt=Izanami-no-Kami}}, {{ill|lt=Konohanasakuyahime|Q1781862|WD=Q1781862|lt=Konohanasakuya-hime-no-Kami}}) *Shinmei Shrine ({{ill|lt=Amaterasu|UNKNOWN|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|WD=Q455602}}) === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:くくみおひこしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kukumiohiko Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kukumiohiko Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Kukumiohiko Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mino Province]] [[Category:岐阜県の神社]] [[Category:美濃国の式内社]] [[Category:岐阜県の旧郷社]] [[Category:養老町の歴史]] [[Category:養老町の建築物]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:qqq]]

Shinto|ja|神道指令|zh|神道指令|WD=Q3029647|1=Shinto Directive}} == P14005 (P14005) == * {{ill|Q11504610|ltq=Q11504610|WD=Q11504610|1=Unranked}} * {{ill|Q11071125|ltq=Q11071125|WD=Q11071125|1=Junior Fifth Rank}} == country (P17) == * {{ill|Q17|ltq=Q17|WD=Q17|1=Japan}} == name in kana (P1814) == * くくみおひこじんじゃ == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '岐阜県養老郡養老町沢田1341', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:久久美雄彦神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11368919}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称=Kukumiohiko Shrine |画像=[[File:Kukumiohiko shrine 2.jpg|250px]]
Torii |所在地={{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Yōrō District|Q1155060|WD=Q1155060|1=Yōrō District, Gifu}}{{ill|lt=Yōrō|Q153814|WD=Q153814|1=Yōrō, Gifu}} Sawada 1341 |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神=Kukumiohiko-no-Kami |社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small) candidate shrine, former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |創建=Unknown |本殿= |例祭=October 8 |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Gifu|label=Kukumiohiko
Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Kukumiohiko Shrine''' (くくみおひこじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Yōrō|Q153814|WD=Q153814|1=Yōrō, Gifu}}, {{ill|lt=Yōrō District|Q1155060|WD=Q1155060|1=Yōrō District, Gifu}}, {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}. It is the Kukumiohiko Shrine of {{ill|lt=Tagi District|Q11431050|WD=Q11431050|1=Tagi district}} in {{ill|lt=Mino Province|Q864156|WD=Q864156|1=Mino Province}} listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Engishiki|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, and its former rank under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. It is also written as Kugumiohiko Shrine. It stands along the {{ill|lt=Tōkai Nature Trail|Q4249812|WD=Q4249812|1=Tōkai Nature Trail}}, on the mid-slope near the northern end of the {{ill|lt=Yōrō Mountains|Q8062668|WD=Q8062668|1=Yōrō Mountains}}. === History === The founding period is unknown. Because the ''{{ill|lt=Shoku Nihon Kōki|Q1041301|WD=Q1041301|1=Shoku Nihon Kōki}}'' records that it received the divine rank of Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade in {{ill|lt=Jōwa (Japan)|Q1075605|WD=Q1075605|lt=Jōwa|1=Jōwa (Heian period)}} 5 ({{ill|lt=838|Q23838|WD=Q23838|1=838}}) from {{ill|lt=Emperor Ninmyō|Q349271|WD=Q349271|1=Emperor Ninmyō}}, it is presumed to have been founded no later than the early {{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|WD=Q193292|1=Heian period}}. There are records that during the {{ill|lt=Sengoku period (Japan)|Q204023|WD=Q204023|lt=Sengoku period|1=Sengoku period}} it was called Kugumihohiko Daimyōjin, and in the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}} it was called Kugumiohiko Jinja Daimyōjin. When it became a gōsha in {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 6 ({{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}}), it was renamed Kukumiohiko Shrine. === Enshrined deity === *Kukumiohiko-no-Kami :What kind of deity Kukumiohiko-no-Kami is remains unclear. The following theories exist: #The same deity as Tatamihiko-no-Mikoto of {{ill|lt=Ibuki Shrine|Q11379321|WD=Q11379321|1=Ibuki Shrine}}. However, deities of Ibuki Shrine also have multiple theories, including {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yamata no Orochi|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto|Q10940411|WD=Q10940411|1=Amenohoakari}}, and {{ill|lt=Ugayafukiaezu|Q1373935|WD=Q1373935|lt=Kusayafugō-no-Mikoto|1=Ugayafukiaezu}}; Tatamihiko-no-Mikoto (also called Ebisudake-no-Kami, Kibuki-no-O-no-Kami, or Ibuki-no-Kami) is regarded as the deity of {{ill|lt=Mount Ibuki|Q2624360|WD=Q2624360|1=Mount Ibuki}}. #Kukumihohiko-no-Kami, a divided deity of Ama-no-Tobeno-Mikoto. Details of Ama-no-Tobeno-Mikoto are unknown, but it is presumed to be {{ill|lt=Ōtonoji and Ōtonobe|Q11073597|WD=Q11073597|lt=Ōtobe-no-Mikoto|1=Otonoji and Otonobe}}. === Precinct shrines === *Sansha Shrine ({{ill|lt=Izanagi|Q813858|WD=Q813858|lt=Izanagi-no-Kami|1=Izanagi}}, {{ill|lt=Izanami|Q682306|WD=Q682306|lt=Izanami-no-Kami|1=Izanami}}, {{ill|lt=Konohanasakuyahime|Q1781862|WD=Q1781862|lt=Konohanasakuya-hime-no-Kami|1=Konohanasakuya-hime}}) *Shinmei Shrine ({{ill|lt=Amaterasu|UNKNOWN|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|WD=Q455602|1=Amaterasu}}) === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:くくみおひこしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kukumiohiko Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kukumiohiko Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Kukumiohiko Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mino Province]] [[Category:岐阜県の神社]] [[Category:美濃国の式内社]] [[Category:岐阜県の旧郷社]] [[Category:養老町の歴史]] [[Category:養老町の建築物]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:qqq]]

Rank}} == country (P17) == * {{ill|Q17|ltq=Q17|WD=Q17|1=Japan}} == name in kana (P1814) == * くくみおひこじんじゃ == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '岐阜県養老郡養老町沢田1341', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:久久美雄彦神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11368919}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称=Kukumiohiko Shrine |画像=[[File:Kukumiohiko shrine 2.jpg|250px]]
Torii |所在地={{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Yōrō District|Q1155060|WD=Q1155060|1=Yōrō District, Gifu}}{{ill|lt=Yōrō|Q153814|WD=Q153814|1=Yōrō, Gifu}} Sawada 1341 |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神=Kukumiohiko-no-Kami |社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small) candidate shrine, former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |創建=Unknown |本殿= |例祭=October 8 |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Gifu|label=Kukumiohiko
Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Kukumiohiko Shrine''' (くくみおひこじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Yōrō|Q153814|WD=Q153814|1=Yōrō, Gifu}}, {{ill|lt=Yōrō District|Q1155060|WD=Q1155060|1=Yōrō District, Gifu}}, {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}. It is the Kukumiohiko Shrine of {{ill|lt=Tagi District|Q11431050|WD=Q11431050|1=Tagi district}} in {{ill|lt=Mino Province|Q864156|WD=Q864156|1=Mino Province}} listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Engishiki|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, and its former rank under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. It is also written as Kugumiohiko Shrine. It stands along the {{ill|lt=Tōkai Nature Trail|Q4249812|WD=Q4249812|1=Tōkai Nature Trail}}, on the mid-slope near the northern end of the {{ill|lt=Yōrō Mountains|Q8062668|WD=Q8062668|1=Yōrō Mountains}}. === History === The founding period is unknown. Because the ''{{ill|lt=Shoku Nihon Kōki|Q1041301|WD=Q1041301|1=Shoku Nihon Kōki}}'' records that it received the divine rank of Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade in {{ill|lt=Jōwa (Japan)|Q1075605|WD=Q1075605|lt=Jōwa|1=Jōwa (Heian period)}} 5 ({{ill|lt=838|Q23838|WD=Q23838|1=838}}) from {{ill|lt=Emperor Ninmyō|Q349271|WD=Q349271|1=Emperor Ninmyō}}, it is presumed to have been founded no later than the early {{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|WD=Q193292|1=Heian period}}. There are records that during the {{ill|lt=Sengoku period (Japan)|Q204023|WD=Q204023|lt=Sengoku period|1=Sengoku period}} it was called Kugumihohiko Daimyōjin, and in the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}} it was called Kugumiohiko Jinja Daimyōjin. When it became a gōsha in {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 6 ({{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}}), it was renamed Kukumiohiko Shrine. === Enshrined deity === *Kukumiohiko-no-Kami :What kind of deity Kukumiohiko-no-Kami is remains unclear. The following theories exist: #The same deity as Tatamihiko-no-Mikoto of {{ill|lt=Ibuki Shrine|Q11379321|WD=Q11379321|1=Ibuki Shrine}}. However, deities of Ibuki Shrine also have multiple theories, including {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yamata no Orochi|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto|Q10940411|WD=Q10940411|1=Amenohoakari}}, and {{ill|lt=Ugayafukiaezu|Q1373935|WD=Q1373935|lt=Kusayafugō-no-Mikoto|1=Ugayafukiaezu}}; Tatamihiko-no-Mikoto (also called Ebisudake-no-Kami, Kibuki-no-O-no-Kami, or Ibuki-no-Kami) is regarded as the deity of {{ill|lt=Mount Ibuki|Q2624360|WD=Q2624360|1=Mount Ibuki}}. #Kukumihohiko-no-Kami, a divided deity of Ama-no-Tobeno-Mikoto. Details of Ama-no-Tobeno-Mikoto are unknown, but it is presumed to be {{ill|lt=Ōtonoji and Ōtonobe|Q11073597|WD=Q11073597|lt=Ōtobe-no-Mikoto|1=Otonoji and Otonobe}}. === Precinct shrines === *Sansha Shrine ({{ill|lt=Izanagi|Q813858|WD=Q813858|lt=Izanagi-no-Kami|1=Izanagi}}, {{ill|lt=Izanami|Q682306|WD=Q682306|lt=Izanami-no-Kami|1=Izanami}}, {{ill|lt=Konohanasakuyahime|Q1781862|WD=Q1781862|lt=Konohanasakuya-hime-no-Kami|1=Konohanasakuya-hime}}) *Shinmei Shrine ({{ill|lt=Amaterasu|UNKNOWN|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|WD=Q455602|1=Amaterasu}}) === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:くくみおひこしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kukumiohiko Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kukumiohiko Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Kukumiohiko Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mino Province]] [[Category:岐阜県の神社]] [[Category:美濃国の式内社]] [[Category:岐阜県の旧郷社]] [[Category:養老町の歴史]] [[Category:養老町の建築物]] [[Category:pages]]

{{wikidata link|Q11368919}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称=Kukumiohiko Shrine |画像=[[File:Kukumiohiko shrine 2.jpg|250px]]
Torii |所在地={{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Yōrō District|Q1155060|WD=Q1155060|1=Yōrō District, Gifu}}{{ill|lt=Yōrō|Q153814|WD=Q153814|1=Yōrō, Gifu}} Sawada 1341 |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神=Kukumiohiko-no-Kami |社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small) candidate shrine, former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |創建=Unknown |本殿= |例祭=October 8 |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Gifu|label=Kukumiohiko
Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Kukumiohiko Shrine''' (くくみおひこじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Yōrō|Q153814|WD=Q153814|1=Yōrō, Gifu}}, {{ill|lt=Yōrō District|Q1155060|WD=Q1155060|1=Yōrō District, Gifu}}, {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}. It is the Kukumiohiko Shrine of {{ill|lt=Tagi District|Q11431050|WD=Q11431050|1=Tagi district}} in {{ill|lt=Mino Province|Q864156|WD=Q864156|1=Mino Province}} listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Engishiki|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, and its former rank under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. It is also written as Kugumiohiko Shrine. It stands along the {{ill|lt=Tōkai Nature Trail|Q4249812|WD=Q4249812|1=Tōkai Nature Trail}}, on the mid-slope near the northern end of the {{ill|lt=Yōrō Mountains|Q8062668|WD=Q8062668|1=Yōrō Mountains}}. === History === The founding period is unknown. Because the ''{{ill|lt=Shoku Nihon Kōki|Q1041301|WD=Q1041301|1=Shoku Nihon Kōki}}'' records that it received the divine rank of Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade in {{ill|lt=Jōwa (Japan)|Q1075605|WD=Q1075605|lt=Jōwa|1=Jōwa (Heian period)}} 5 ({{ill|lt=838|Q23838|WD=Q23838|1=838}}) from {{ill|lt=Emperor Ninmyō|Q349271|WD=Q349271|1=Emperor Ninmyō}}, it is presumed to have been founded no later than the early {{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|WD=Q193292|1=Heian period}}. There are records that during the {{ill|lt=Sengoku period (Japan)|Q204023|WD=Q204023|lt=Sengoku period|1=Sengoku period}} it was called Kugumihohiko Daimyōjin, and in the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}} it was called Kugumiohiko Jinja Daimyōjin. When it became a gōsha in {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 6 ({{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}}), it was renamed Kukumiohiko Shrine. === Enshrined deity === *Kukumiohiko-no-Kami :What kind of deity Kukumiohiko-no-Kami is remains unclear. The following theories exist: #The same deity as Tatamihiko-no-Mikoto of {{ill|lt=Ibuki Shrine|Q11379321|WD=Q11379321|1=Ibuki Shrine}}. However, deities of Ibuki Shrine also have multiple theories, including {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yamata no Orochi|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto|Q10940411|WD=Q10940411|1=Amenohoakari}}, and {{ill|lt=Ugayafukiaezu|Q1373935|WD=Q1373935|lt=Kusayafugō-no-Mikoto|1=Ugayafukiaezu}}; Tatamihiko-no-Mikoto (also called Ebisudake-no-Kami, Kibuki-no-O-no-Kami, or Ibuki-no-Kami) is regarded as the deity of {{ill|lt=Mount Ibuki|Q2624360|WD=Q2624360|1=Mount Ibuki}}. #Kukumihohiko-no-Kami, a divided deity of Ama-no-Tobeno-Mikoto. Details of Ama-no-Tobeno-Mikoto are unknown, but it is presumed to be {{ill|lt=Ōtonoji and Ōtonobe|Q11073597|WD=Q11073597|lt=Ōtobe-no-Mikoto|1=Otonoji and Otonobe}}. === Precinct shrines === *Sansha Shrine ({{ill|lt=Izanagi|Q813858|WD=Q813858|lt=Izanagi-no-Kami|1=Izanagi}}, {{ill|lt=Izanami|Q682306|WD=Q682306|lt=Izanami-no-Kami|1=Izanami}}, {{ill|lt=Konohanasakuyahime|Q1781862|WD=Q1781862|lt=Konohanasakuya-hime-no-Kami|1=Konohanasakuya-hime}}) *Shinmei Shrine ({{ill|lt=Amaterasu|UNKNOWN|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|WD=Q455602|1=Amaterasu}}) === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kukumiohiko Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kukumiohiko Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Kukumiohiko Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mino Province]] [[Category:岐阜県の神社]] [[Category:美濃国の式内社]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:養老町の歴史]] [[Category:養老町の建築物]] [[Category:pages]]

{{wikidata link|Q11368919}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称=Kukumiohiko Shrine |画像=[[File:Kukumiohiko shrine 2.jpg|250px]]
Torii |所在地={{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Yōrō District|Q1155060|WD=Q1155060|1=Yōrō District, Gifu}}{{ill|lt=Yōrō|Q153814|WD=Q153814|1=Yōrō, Gifu}} Sawada 1341 |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神=Kukumiohiko-no-Kami |社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small) candidate shrine, former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |創建=Unknown |本殿= |例祭=October 8 |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Gifu|label=Kukumiohiko
Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Kukumiohiko Shrine''' (くくみおひこじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Yōrō|Q153814|WD=Q153814|1=Yōrō, Gifu}}, {{ill|lt=Yōrō District|Q1155060|WD=Q1155060|1=Yōrō District, Gifu}}, {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}. It is the Kukumiohiko Shrine of {{ill|lt=Tagi District|Q11431050|WD=Q11431050|1=Tagi district}} in {{ill|lt=Mino Province|Q864156|WD=Q864156|1=Mino Province}} listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Engishiki|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, and its former rank under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. It is also written as Kugumiohiko Shrine. It stands along the {{ill|lt=Tōkai Nature Trail|Q4249812|WD=Q4249812|1=Tōkai Nature Trail}}, on the mid-slope near the northern end of the {{ill|lt=Yōrō Mountains|Q8062668|WD=Q8062668|1=Yōrō Mountains}}. === History === The founding period is unknown. Because the ''{{ill|lt=Shoku Nihon Kōki|Q1041301|WD=Q1041301|1=Shoku Nihon Kōki}}'' records that it received the divine rank of Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade in {{ill|lt=Jōwa (Japan)|Q1075605|WD=Q1075605|lt=Jōwa|1=Jōwa (Heian period)}} 5 ({{ill|lt=838|Q23838|WD=Q23838|1=838}}) from {{ill|lt=Emperor Ninmyō|Q349271|WD=Q349271|1=Emperor Ninmyō}}, it is presumed to have been founded no later than the early {{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|WD=Q193292|1=Heian period}}. There are records that during the {{ill|lt=Sengoku period (Japan)|Q204023|WD=Q204023|lt=Sengoku period|1=Sengoku period}} it was called Kugumihohiko Daimyōjin, and in the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}} it was called Kugumiohiko Jinja Daimyōjin. When it became a gōsha in {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 6 ({{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}}), it was renamed Kukumiohiko Shrine. === Enshrined deity === *Kukumiohiko-no-Kami :What kind of deity Kukumiohiko-no-Kami is remains unclear. The following theories exist: #The same deity as Tatamihiko-no-Mikoto of {{ill|lt=Ibuki Shrine|Q11379321|WD=Q11379321|1=Ibuki Shrine}}. However, deities of Ibuki Shrine also have multiple theories, including {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yamata no Orochi|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto|Q10940411|WD=Q10940411|1=Amenohoakari}}, and {{ill|lt=Ugayafukiaezu|Q1373935|WD=Q1373935|lt=Kusayafugō-no-Mikoto|1=Ugayafukiaezu}}; Tatamihiko-no-Mikoto (also called Ebisudake-no-Kami, Kibuki-no-O-no-Kami, or Ibuki-no-Kami) is regarded as the deity of {{ill|lt=Mount Ibuki|Q2624360|WD=Q2624360|1=Mount Ibuki}}. #Kukumihohiko-no-Kami, a divided deity of Ama-no-Tobeno-Mikoto. Details of Ama-no-Tobeno-Mikoto are unknown, but it is presumed to be {{ill|lt=Ōtonoji and Ōtonobe|Q11073597|WD=Q11073597|lt=Ōtobe-no-Mikoto|1=Otonoji and Otonobe}}. === Precinct shrines === *Sansha Shrine ({{ill|lt=Izanagi|Q813858|WD=Q813858|lt=Izanagi-no-Kami|1=Izanagi}}, {{ill|lt=Izanami|Q682306|WD=Q682306|lt=Izanami-no-Kami|1=Izanami}}, {{ill|lt=Konohanasakuyahime|Q1781862|WD=Q1781862|lt=Konohanasakuya-hime-no-Kami|1=Konohanasakuya-hime}}) *Shinmei Shrine ({{ill|lt=Amaterasu|UNKNOWN|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|WD=Q455602|1=Amaterasu}}) === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kukumiohiko Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kukumiohiko Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Kukumiohiko Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mino Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mino Province]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:養老町の歴史]] [[Category:養老町の建築物]] [[Category:pages]]

Rank}} == country (P17) == * {{ill|Q17|ltq=Q17|WD=Q17|1=Japan}} == name in kana (P1814) == * くくみおひこじんじゃ == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '岐阜県養老郡養老町沢田1341', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:久久美雄彦神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11368919}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称=Kukumiohiko Shrine |画像=[[File:Kukumiohiko shrine 2.jpg|250px]]
Torii |所在地={{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Yōrō District|Q1155060|WD=Q1155060|1=Yōrō District, Gifu}}{{ill|lt=Yōrō|Q153814|WD=Q153814|1=Yōrō, Gifu}} Sawada 1341 |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神=Kukumiohiko-no-Kami |社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small) candidate shrine, former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |創建=Unknown |本殿= |例祭=October 8 |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Gifu|label=Kukumiohiko
Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Kukumiohiko Shrine''' (くくみおひこじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Yōrō|Q153814|WD=Q153814|1=Yōrō, Gifu}}, {{ill|lt=Yōrō District|Q1155060|WD=Q1155060|1=Yōrō District, Gifu}}, {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}. It is the Kukumiohiko Shrine of {{ill|lt=Tagi District|Q11431050|WD=Q11431050|1=Tagi district}} in {{ill|lt=Mino Province|Q864156|WD=Q864156|1=Mino Province}} listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Engishiki|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, and its former rank under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. It is also written as Kugumiohiko Shrine. It stands along the {{ill|lt=Tōkai Nature Trail|Q4249812|WD=Q4249812|1=Tōkai Nature Trail}}, on the mid-slope near the northern end of the {{ill|lt=Yōrō Mountains|Q8062668|WD=Q8062668|1=Yōrō Mountains}}. === History === The founding period is unknown. Because the ''{{ill|lt=Shoku Nihon Kōki|Q1041301|WD=Q1041301|1=Shoku Nihon Kōki}}'' records that it received the divine rank of Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade in {{ill|lt=Jōwa (Japan)|Q1075605|WD=Q1075605|lt=Jōwa|1=Jōwa (Heian period)}} 5 ({{ill|lt=838|Q23838|WD=Q23838|1=838}}) from {{ill|lt=Emperor Ninmyō|Q349271|WD=Q349271|1=Emperor Ninmyō}}, it is presumed to have been founded no later than the early {{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|WD=Q193292|1=Heian period}}. There are records that during the {{ill|lt=Sengoku period (Japan)|Q204023|WD=Q204023|lt=Sengoku period|1=Sengoku period}} it was called Kugumihohiko Daimyōjin, and in the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}} it was called Kugumiohiko Jinja Daimyōjin. When it became a gōsha in {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 6 ({{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}}), it was renamed Kukumiohiko Shrine. === Enshrined deity === *Kukumiohiko-no-Kami :What kind of deity Kukumiohiko-no-Kami is remains unclear. The following theories exist: #The same deity as Tatamihiko-no-Mikoto of {{ill|lt=Ibuki Shrine|Q11379321|WD=Q11379321|1=Ibuki Shrine}}. However, deities of Ibuki Shrine also have multiple theories, including {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yamata no Orochi|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto|Q10940411|WD=Q10940411|1=Amenohoakari}}, and {{ill|lt=Ugayafukiaezu|Q1373935|WD=Q1373935|lt=Kusayafugō-no-Mikoto|1=Ugayafukiaezu}}; Tatamihiko-no-Mikoto (also called Ebisudake-no-Kami, Kibuki-no-O-no-Kami, or Ibuki-no-Kami) is regarded as the deity of {{ill|lt=Mount Ibuki|Q2624360|WD=Q2624360|1=Mount Ibuki}}. #Kukumihohiko-no-Kami, a divided deity of Ama-no-Tobeno-Mikoto. Details of Ama-no-Tobeno-Mikoto are unknown, but it is presumed to be {{ill|lt=Ōtonoji and Ōtonobe|Q11073597|WD=Q11073597|lt=Ōtobe-no-Mikoto|1=Otonoji and Otonobe}}. === Precinct shrines === *Sansha Shrine ({{ill|lt=Izanagi|Q813858|WD=Q813858|lt=Izanagi-no-Kami|1=Izanagi}}, {{ill|lt=Izanami|Q682306|WD=Q682306|lt=Izanami-no-Kami|1=Izanami}}, {{ill|lt=Konohanasakuyahime|Q1781862|WD=Q1781862|lt=Konohanasakuya-hime-no-Kami|1=Konohanasakuya-hime}}) *Shinmei Shrine ({{ill|lt=Amaterasu|UNKNOWN|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|WD=Q455602|1=Amaterasu}}) === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kukumiohiko Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kukumiohiko Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kukumiohiko Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha in Mino Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mino Province]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:養老町の歴史]] [[Category:養老町の建築物]]

{{wikidata link|Q11368919}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称=Kukumiohiko Shrine |画像=[[File:Kukumiohiko shrine 2.jpg|250px]]
Torii |所在地={{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|qid=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Yōrō District|Q1155060|qid=Q1155060|1=Yōrō District, Gifu}}{{ill|lt=Yōrō|Q153814|qid=Q153814|1=Yōrō, Gifu}} Sawada 1341 |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神=Kukumiohiko-no-Kami |社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}} (small) candidate shrine, former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|qid=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |創建=Unknown |本殿= |例祭=October 8 |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Gifu|label=Kukumiohiko
Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Kukumiohiko Shrine''' (くくみおひこじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Yōrō|Q153814|qid=Q153814|1=Yōrō, Gifu}}, {{ill|lt=Yōrō District|Q1155060|qid=Q1155060|1=Yōrō District, Gifu}}, {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|qid=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}. It is the Kukumiohiko Shrine of {{ill|lt=Tagi District|Q11431050|qid=Q11431050|1=Tagi district}} in {{ill|lt=Mino Province|Q864156|qid=Q864156|1=Mino Province}} listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|qid=Q11064932|lt=Engishiki|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, and its former rank under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|qid=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|qid=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. It is also written as Kugumiohiko Shrine. It stands along the {{ill|lt=Tōkai Nature Trail|Q4249812|qid=Q4249812|1=Tōkai Nature Trail}}, on the mid-slope near the northern end of the {{ill|lt=Yōrō Mountains|Q8062668|qid=Q8062668|1=Yōrō Mountains}}. === History === The founding period is unknown. Because the ''{{ill|lt=Shoku Nihon Kōki|Q1041301|qid=Q1041301|1=Shoku Nihon Kōki}}'' records that it received the divine rank of Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade in {{ill|lt=Jōwa (Japan)|Q1075605|qid=Q1075605|lt=Jōwa|1=Jōwa (Heian period)}} 5 ({{ill|lt=838|Q23838|qid=Q23838|1=838}}) from {{ill|lt=Emperor Ninmyō|Q349271|qid=Q349271|1=Emperor Ninmyō}}, it is presumed to have been founded no later than the early {{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|qid=Q193292|1=Heian period}}. There are records that during the {{ill|lt=Sengoku period (Japan)|Q204023|qid=Q204023|lt=Sengoku period|1=Sengoku period}} it was called Kugumihohiko Daimyōjin, and in the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|qid=Q184963|1=Edo period}} it was called Kugumiohiko Jinja Daimyōjin. When it became a gōsha in {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|qid=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 6 ({{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|qid=Q7757|1=1873}}), it was renamed Kukumiohiko Shrine. === Enshrined deity === *Kukumiohiko-no-Kami :What kind of deity Kukumiohiko-no-Kami is remains unclear. The following theories exist: #The same deity as Tatamihiko-no-Mikoto of {{ill|lt=Ibuki Shrine|Q11379321|qid=Q11379321|1=Ibuki Shrine}}. However, deities of Ibuki Shrine also have multiple theories, including {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yamata no Orochi|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Hoakari-no-Mikoto|Q10940411|qid=Q10940411|1=Amenohoakari}}, and {{ill|lt=Ugayafukiaezu|Q1373935|qid=Q1373935|lt=Kusayafugō-no-Mikoto|1=Ugayafukiaezu}}; Tatamihiko-no-Mikoto (also called Ebisudake-no-Kami, Kibuki-no-O-no-Kami, or Ibuki-no-Kami) is regarded as the deity of {{ill|lt=Mount Ibuki|Q2624360|qid=Q2624360|1=Mount Ibuki}}. #Kukumihohiko-no-Kami, a divided deity of Ama-no-Tobeno-Mikoto. Details of Ama-no-Tobeno-Mikoto are unknown, but it is presumed to be {{ill|lt=Ōtonoji and Ōtonobe|Q11073597|qid=Q11073597|lt=Ōtobe-no-Mikoto|1=Otonoji and Otonobe}}. === Precinct shrines === *Sansha Shrine ({{ill|lt=Izanagi|Q813858|qid=Q813858|lt=Izanagi-no-Kami|1=Izanagi}}, {{ill|lt=Izanami|Q682306|qid=Q682306|lt=Izanami-no-Kami|1=Izanami}}, {{ill|lt=Konohanasakuyahime|Q1781862|qid=Q1781862|lt=Konohanasakuya-hime-no-Kami|1=Konohanasakuya-hime}}) *Shinmei Shrine ({{ill|lt=Amaterasu|UNKNOWN|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|qid=Q455602|1=Amaterasu}}) === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kukumiohiko Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kukumiohiko Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kukumiohiko Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha in Mino Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mino Province]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:養老町の歴史]] [[Category:養老町の建築物]]
